summary: The satisfies/graph joins key on the bare member name, not the compiled `kind:name` label, so a cross-kind name collision cross-attributes edges into wrong findings, not silence

edit.description: `SatisfiesRow.member` (drift.rs:2313) carries a bare id where `MentionRow` already carries the `kind:name` address (identity is a compiled label — pipeline.md, 'The lock'). Qualify the wire to `kind:name`; `derive_layout_rows` (drift.rs:1082, has `host_address()` in hand) writes the qualified label for layout-sourced satisfies. Read robustly (0024): a pre-fix lock's bare rows qualify against the corpus where unambiguous, the next emit rewrites the file canonical; a true cross-kind collision stays the malformed lock the spec names (0021) — refused loud.
`satisfiesRows` (declarations.ts:314) pushes `{ member: member.name }` — the bare name; write the `${member.kind}:${member.name}` address the way `mentionRows` already does (declarations.ts:334).

`resolve_kind_units`' `row.member == unit.id` loop (main.rs:1019/1022) runs per kind — the collision leak; fold on the qualified label so a same-named member of another kind no longer cross-attributes.

`author_satisfies` (tests/common/mod.rs:175, plus its rule-kind wrapper at 427) round-trips satisfies fixtures through the wire — the shared-helper fan-out; update it and its callers to the qualified label so the source→row→read cycle stays consistent.
Satisfies-fixture assertions that read back the bare member id move to the `kind:name` label.

tests.asserts: two kinds sharing a member name, each with a qualified satisfies row binding only its own kind's member, produce no cross-attributed requirement.kind refusals or false zero-degree findings
satisfies rows carry the `kind:name` label, not the bare member name; a pre-fix bare row qualifies where unambiguous and a cross-kind bare collision is refused as a malformed lock

acceptance: A member name shared across two kinds no longer cross-attributes satisfies/graph edges; each qualified row binds only its own kind's member; a pre-fix lock's bare rows qualify where unambiguous and a cross-kind collision is refused as a malformed lock.
notes: Live repro (centercode 07-15): a dev-pack `csharp` beside a rule `csharp` cross-attributed satisfies+mention edges — false requirement.kind refusals both ways, false zero-degree, clean bisect on rename. Posture ruled by 0024 (read robustly; cross-kind collision malformed-loud) — dependsOnForks dropped. Fan-out: tests/common/mod.rs author_satisfies, tests/emit.rs. observed 0aa9e62; scoped 06e0c2c (re-verified vs 06c44b1).

summary: A post-e7b859a engine's first emit against an older `./`-spelled lock silently mass-reaps every live projection — the orphan sweep matches no row and reaps all

edit.description: The orphan sweep (drift.rs:865-872) builds `owned_paths` via `to_lock_path` (drift.rs:501, backslash-only normalization) but compares against `read_prior_provenance` rows' raw `source_path` (drift.rs:866); an older lock spells them `./CLAUDE.md` while the post-e7b859a fingerprint pass keys the normalized `CLAUDE.md`, so every live projection reads ownerless and is reaped. Read robustly (0024): normalize both sides of the join — canonicalize the row `source_path` too (strip `./`, the backslash handling as precedent) so a byte-identical projection matches its row. The genuine total-reap backstop is EMIT-INTO-REROOT-REAP's cliff refusal.

tests.asserts: an emit against a lock whose provenance rows carry `./`-prefixed source paths matches them to the live projections and reaps nothing (0 reaped), rather than mass-reaping every projection

acceptance: An emit under a normalized-path engine against an older `./`-spelled lock matches each byte-identical projection to its row and reaps nothing — no silent mass-reap; the loud-or-nothing invariant holds.
notes: Field incident (centercode 07-15): one emit reported '21 reaped', exit 0 — deleted every projection while writing a lock claiming them live and unchanged. e7b859a normalizes the workspace path pre-`harness_root`; to_lock_path (drift.rs:501) strips backslashes, never `./`. 0024 rules read-robustly (normalize the join key) — dependsOnForks dropped; serialized after SATISFIES (shared drift.rs). observed 0aa9e62; scoped 06e0c2c (re-verified vs 06c44b1).

summary: `emit --into <dir>` inside an adopted harness re-roots the projection tree, orphaning every live projection — emit refuses the total-reap wave unless the author spells an explicit teardown flag

edit.description: In the emit reap path (`reap_or_report_orphan` drift.rs:1312, driven by `emit_program` drift.rs:537, over the orphan sweep at 865-872) add the cliff refusal (0024): a reap wave that would delete every live projection while emitting nothing refuses with the finding stated, never deletes byte-faithfully. The `--into` re-root of an adopted harness is the motivating instance (every prior projection reads ownerless). The explicit teardown flag is threaded here as the spelled opt-out. Sits on the LOCK-SPELLING-corrected join so the refusal fires only on a genuine wave, not a spelling-induced false one.

Add the teardown flag to Emit and carry it to the reap guard (the `--into` arg already exists: `into: PathBuf` main.rs:134, emit handler main.rs:308-316); with the flag set, a total-reap wave is the author's spelled intent and proceeds. Shares the arg/workspace resolution CHECK-ARG-HALF-GATE reworked (shipped 4256274).

tests.asserts: `emit --into <nested-dir>` inside an adopted harness refuses the total-reap wave (finding stated, projections intact); the explicit teardown flag lets the wave through; plain `emit` still reaps a genuinely single ownerless projection

acceptance: A reap wave that would delete every live projection while emitting nothing refuses with the finding stated — no silent mass-reap on an `--into` re-root — unless the author spells the explicit teardown flag; a genuine single-orphan reap still works.
notes: Observed at aba7e47: `emit --into .temper` reaped 7 live root projections (CLAUDE.md, five rules, a skill); restored via git. 0024's 'refuse at the cliff' — total-reap refusal + explicit teardown flag (PR #20's bare refusal adopted with the spelled escape) — dependsOnForks dropped. Carries the reap-wave half of 0024's 'emit gains the cliff refusals and the teardown flag'; LOCK-LAYER-DROP carries the dropped-layer half. scoped 06e0c2c (re-verified vs 01625fb).

summary: A re-read dropping a whole declared layer the committed lock still carries — a pre-0018 harness's ~57 embedded members gone on re-emit — refuses with the finding stated, not silent loss

edit.description: The second cliff refusal (0024): a re-read (`emit_program` drift.rs:537) that produces zero members for a whole layer the committed lock still declares — declaration rows present in the prior lock, derived rows empty — refuses with the finding stated. Compare this emit's derived declaration rows against `read_prior_provenance`/the prior lock's declared collections; a whole declared collection dropping to zero refuses. The disappearance is loud because the lock still declares it, never because prose was mined (invariant 1). Reuses EMIT-INTO's teardown flag as the spelled opt-out for a genuine layer removal.

tests.asserts: a lock declaring an embedded-member layer whose source re-reads to zero members refuses with the finding stated; the teardown flag lets the removal through; a layer that legitimately loses one of many members still emits

acceptance: A re-read that drops a whole declared layer the committed lock still carries refuses with the finding stated — no silent layer loss on an upgrade — unless the author spells the teardown flag; a partial member change still emits.
notes: 0024's fourth incident: a harness green pre-0018 re-emitted with its ~57 embedded members silently gone (`explain`: 'carries no nested member'). The `(member-fence-dead-text)` loss repro folded in — the prose-scan alternative is rejected (0024 'Rejected': matching-is-mining; a quoted fence false-positives); the loss is the cliff refusal's job. Serialized after EMIT-INTO (shared drift.rs refusal path + teardown flag). observed d26b3ce; scoped 06e0c2c.

summary: A composed mention naming a declared kind with no composed member wrongly fails emit; it should defer to check (the row rides the lock), while a mention naming no declared kind still refuses

edit.description: `renderTextBody` (emit.ts:162-164) rejects any mention whose address is outside `mentionable` ('resolves to no declared value'). Thread the program's declared kinds; defer (emit the mention as a lock row, no throw) when the address's kind is a declared kind with no composed member, keep refusing when the address names no declared kind (pipeline.md 'Refusing': the deferral's signal is the declared kind, never a guess over the address).

Carry the deferred mention as a `MentionRow` riding the lock so check resolves it at the same table `implemented-by` resolves on; `mentionRows` already builds `${member.kind}:${member.name}` addresses (declarations.ts:334). Serialized after SATISFIES (shared file).

Verify the engine resolves a deferred mention edge over discovered members at check time (note: verify graph already covers it) — check owns the verdict; an undeclared-kind address stays dangling at emit.

tests.asserts: a mention addressing a declared kind with no composed member emits (defers) rather than throwing; a mention addressing no declared kind still throws at emit
check resolves a deferred discovery-locus mention against a discovered member of the declared kind, and refuses when no such member is discovered

acceptance: A composed mention naming a declared kind with no composed member emits (defers to check, which resolves it over discovered members) instead of failing emit; a mention naming no declared kind still refuses at emit; the example's skill→script mention demo builds.
notes: pipeline.md 'Emit'/'Refusing' amendment (human-ruled 07-15, 06e0c2c) resolves `(composed-mention-discovery-locus)`. Live: `source:main` is a real corpus member the engine resolves `implemented-by`/`graph.route` on, but `renderTextBody` rejects it as dangling. Unblocks the example's (examples/base-harness) skill→script mention demo. Serialized after SATISFIES (shared sdk/src/declarations.ts). observed d26b3ce; scoped 06e0c2c.

summary: install's managed-by note is a frontmatter `#` comment, so a frontmatterless projection (memory, any frontmatterless kind) carries no in-band banner — grow a block-level HTML-comment form

edit.description: The managed-by note (`NOTE_COMMENT` at install.rs:159, placed/idempotence-keyed by `project_note` at install.rs:1549 via `NOTE_MARKER`) is a `#`-prefixed YAML frontmatter comment. Add a second form — a block-level HTML comment — for a markdown-bodied frontmatterless kind (memory and any frontmatterless custom kind); a frontmatter kind keeps the `#` form. Placement stays content-keyed and idempotent (re-run converges).

tests.asserts: a frontmatterless memory projection carries the block-level HTML-comment managed-by banner and a re-run converges (no duplicate); a frontmatter kind keeps the `#` frontmatter-comment form

acceptance: A frontmatterless projection carries an in-band managed-by banner (a block-level HTML comment); frontmatter kinds keep the `#` form; both forms stay content-keyed and idempotent.
notes: Ruled 07-15 (06e0c2c) resolving `(frontmatterless-managed-by-banner)`. External fact: Claude Code strips block-level HTML comments before injection (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-15) — human-visible, model-invisible. Courtesy, not a guarantee gap: a hand-edit is still hash-caught as drift (pipeline.md 'Drift'). Live: pack-kind field trial, centercode 07-15 — ten `file()` members adopted byte-faithful, no banner. observed d26b3ce; scoped 06e0c2c.

summary: Layout/custom-kind authoring guidance has no written home — add it to the bundled teaching skill (`OPERATE_SKILL`), which teaches mechanics

edit.description: `OPERATE_SKILL` (bundle.rs:68) teaches operate-the-gate mechanics; add concise layout / custom-kind authoring guidance — 'if your host mixes prose and members, declare a layout and author the document' — mechanics, never taste (distribution.md 'What ships': the skill teaches mechanics, opinions live in default contracts). The skill body is byte-faithful-copied by bundle from this constant.

tests.asserts: the bundled operate-the-gate skill body carries the layout / custom-kind authoring guidance

acceptance: The operate-the-gate skill teaches layout/custom-kind authoring as mechanics; blocks nothing.
notes: Ruled 07-15 (06e0c2c) resolving `(custom-kind-consumer-docs)`: layout authoring is mechanics, so it lives in the plugin's teaching skill, never a README/`docs/` horizon page. Verified the skill source exists now — `OPERATE_SKILL` (src/bundle.rs:68), shipped by `bundle` (tests/bundle.rs) — so bundle work is live and the entry is open. observed d26b3ce; scoped 06e0c2c.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. scoped at b676656.
